prompt_text:
### Demographics: 42-year-old Asian man

### Visit type: Follow-up visit (12-month follow-up)

### Reason for visit / current symptoms
Reason for visit: Routine follow-up (every 3 months) | Diagnosis context: 4 years ago Diagnosed with type 2 diabetes during urgent care evaluation for fatigue/blurred vision
Current symptoms: Rare nocturnal hypoglycemia (1 episode in 3 months); no DKA/HHS signs; Intermittent GI discomfort (nausea/bloating); Worsening edema and dyspnea over the last 3 months.

### History
- Other conditions: Chronic kidney disease Stage 2 (60-89)
Heart failure: HFrEF (NYHA Class III)
ASCVD: Ischemic stroke or transient ischemic attack history; Coronary artery disease (History of CABG)
Body mass index category: Overweight
Diabetic retinopathy
Diabetic Retinopathy
Major Depressive Disorder
Psychotic Disorder on Atypical Antipsychotics
Recurrent Complicated Urinary Tract Infections
- Diabetes-related comorbidities: CKD: Stage 2 (60-89); HF: HFrEF (NYHA Class III); ASCVD: Ischemic stroke or transient ischemic attack history; MASLD: Normal; BMI Category: Overweight
- Family history: Type 2 diabetes (mother)
- Social history: Current smoker (15 cigarettes/day for 15 years; ~11.2 pack-years); No alcohol use

### Medication information
- Current medications: Metformin 1000mg twice daily; Dulaglutide 1.5mg once weekly; Glimepiride 2mg once daily; Lobeglitazone 0.5mg once daily; Quetiapine (150 mg nightly), Sertraline (50 mg daily), Rosuvastatin (10 mg daily), Lisinopril (10 mg daily), Ciprofloxacin (500 mg twice daily for 7 days)

### Physical exam (current)
Weight 77.9 kg; Height 180.2 cm; BMI 24.0 kg/m²; Blood pressure 111/62 mmHg

### Laboratory values (with date / relative timing)
Format: YYYY-MM-DD value or relative timing (12 months ago / 9 months ago / 6 months ago / 3 months ago / current)
Order: 12 months ago -> 9 months ago -> 6 months ago -> 3 months ago -> current
- Glycemia: HbA1c (12 months ago 8.7%; 9 months ago 9.4%; 6 months ago 9.9%; 3 months ago 9.4%; current 9.5%), FPG (12 months ago 242mg/dL; 9 months ago 265mg/dL; 6 months ago 267mg/dL; 3 months ago 201mg/dL; current 232mg/dL)
- Anthropometrics / blood pressure: Weight (12 months ago 78.2kg; 9 months ago 77.9kg; 6 months ago 74.2kg; 3 months ago 76.8kg; current 77.9kg), Height (12 months ago 180.2cm; 9 months ago 180.2cm; 6 months ago 180.2cm; 3 months ago 180.2cm; current 180.2cm), BMI (12 months ago 24.0kg/m²; 9 months ago 24.0kg/m²; 6 months ago 23.0kg/m²; 3 months ago 24.0kg/m²; current 24.0kg/m²), SBP (12 months ago 120mmHg; 9 months ago 120mmHg; 6 months ago 120mmHg; 3 months ago 120mmHg; current 111mmHg), DBP (12 months ago 60mmHg; 9 months ago 61mmHg; 6 months ago 62mmHg; 3 months ago 62mmHg; current 62mmHg)
- Liver function: ALT (12 months ago 8U/L; 9 months ago 7U/L; 6 months ago 8.0U/L; 3 months ago 13U/L; current 10U/L), AST (12 months ago 24U/L; 9 months ago 18U/L; 6 months ago 18U/L; 3 months ago 24U/L; current 20U/L), Total Bilirubin (12 months ago 0.7mg/dL; 9 months ago 0.7mg/dL; 6 months ago 0.7mg/dL; 3 months ago 0.7mg/dL; current 0.7mg/dL)
- Kidney: eGFR (12 months ago 77mL/min/1.73m²; 9 months ago 78mL/min/1.73m²; 6 months ago 74mL/min/1.73m²; 3 months ago 78mL/min/1.73m²; current 75mL/min/1.73m²), UACR (12 months ago 23mg/g; 9 months ago 30mg/g; 6 months ago 23mg/g; 3 months ago 29mg/g; current 28mg/g)
- Lipids: LDL (12 months ago 73mg/dL; 9 months ago 69mg/dL; 6 months ago 72mg/dL; 3 months ago 72mg/dL; current 70mg/dL); HDL (12 months ago 54mg/dL; 9 months ago 54mg/dL; 6 months ago 52mg/dL; 3 months ago 51mg/dL; current 53mg/dL); Triglycerides (12 months ago 121mg/dL; 9 months ago 142mg/dL; 6 months ago 120mg/dL; 3 months ago 144mg/dL; current 132mg/dL)
- Hemoglobin: Hemoglobin (12 months ago 15.0g/dL; 9 months ago 15.0g/dL; 6 months ago 14.2g/dL; 3 months ago 14.9g/dL; current 15.0g/dL)
- Additional tests: urine ketone Negative (Current); fasting C-peptide Not measured (Current)

### Individualized treatment decision factors
Current adherence and self-management: Very poor adherence (estimated <50%); SMBG rarely performed; lifestyle control severely limited.
Financial context: Patient has comprehensive insurance with minimal out-of-pocket burden and reports no financial barriers to clinically indicated therapies (budget ≤ KRW 1,000,000/month).
